revision.text:
[[ja:石坐神社]] {{Expand Japanese|石坐神社}} {{Infobox religious building | name = Iwai Shrine (Q85882705) | native_name = 石坐神社 | image = Iwai Shrine.jpg | mapframe = yes | mapframe-zoom = 15 | mapframe-wikidata = yes | mapframe-point = none | map_type = Japan | map_caption = Iwai Shrine (Q85882705) | coordinates = {{coord|35.000305|135.888094}} | map_relief = 1 | religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]] | website = {{Official website|http://www.iwaijinzya.org/}} }} {{nihongo|'''Iwai Shrine'''|石坐神社}} is a [[shinto shrine]] in the [[Engishiki Jinmyōchō]]. It is located in [[Ōmi Province]]. == instance of (P31) == * {{ill|Shinto shrine|lt=Shinto shrine|ar|جينجا|arz|ضريح شنتو|bcl|Shinto shrine|ca|Jinja|cs|Šintoistická svatyně|da|Shinto-tempel|de|Shintō-Schrein|el|Σιντοϊστικό Ιερό|en|Shinto shrine|eo|Ŝintoa sanktejo|es|Jinja|eu|Jinja|fa|معبد شینتویی|fi|Šintolainen pyhäkkö|fr|Sanctuaire shinto|hi|शिंतो मंदिर|hr|Šintoistički hram|hu|Sintó szentély|id|Jinja|it|Jinja|ja|神社|ka|ძინძია|ko|신사 (신토)|lb|Shintō-Schräin|mr|शिंतो मंदिर|nl|Jinja (heiligdom)|no|Jinja|or|ଜିଞ୍ଜା|pl|Jinja|pt|Templo xintoísta|ru|Дзиндзя|sc|Jinja|simple|Shinto shrine|sl|Šintoistično svetišče|sr|Шинтоистички храм|sv|Jinja|th|ศาลเจ้าชินโต|tr|Şinto tapınağı|uk|Святилище (синто)|vi|Thần xã|wuu|神社|zh_yue|神社|zh|神社|qid=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} * {{ill|Shikinaisha|lt=Shikinaisha|en|Shikinaisha|zh|式內社|qid=Q134917286|1=Shikinaisha}} * {{ill|Shikinai Shosha|lt=Shikinai Shōsha|en|Shikinai Shosha|ja|式内小社|simple|Shikinai Shosha|qid=Q134917287|1=Shikinai Shosha}} * {{ill|Q135160342|ltq=Q135160342|qid=Q135160342|1=Kokuhei-sha}} * {{ill|Q135026601|ltq=Q135026601|qid=Q135026601|1=Shikinai Hiteisha}} == part of (P361) == * {{ill|Q11638477|ltq=Q11638477|qid=Q11638477|1=List of Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province}} ** Series ordinal: 3 ** P155: {{ill|Shitorino Shrine|lt=Shitorino Shrine|qid=Q135039785|1=Shitorino Shrine (Q135039785)}} ** P156: {{ill|Q135039789|ltq=Q135039789|qid=Q135039789|1=Kantano Shrine (Q135039789)}} == located in the administrative territorial entity (P131) == * {{ill|Q202907|ltq=Q202907|qid=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}} * {{ill|Ōmi Province|lt=Ōmi Province|ca|Província d'Ōmi|ckb|پارێزگای ئۆمی|cs|Provincie Ómi|de|Provinz Ōmi|en|Ōmi Province|es|Provincia de Ōmi|fa|ولایت اومی|fi|Ōmin provinssi|fr|Province d'Ōmi|gl|Provincia de Ōmi|hak|Ōmi-koet|id|Provinsi Ōmi|it|Ōmi|ja|近江国|ko|오미국|lt|Omio žemė|nl|Omi (provincie)|pl|Prowincja Ōmi|pt|Província de Ōmi|ru|Оми (провинция)|simple|Ōmi Province|sk|Ómi (provincia)|sr|Провинција Оми|tr|Ōmi (vilayet)|uk|Провінція Омі|vi|Tỉnh Ōmi|zh_min_nan|Ômi-kok|zh_yue|近江國|zh|近江國|qid=Q843882|1=Ōmi Province}} * {{ill|Q7496289|ltq=Q7496289|qid=Q7496289|1=Shiga District, Shiga}} == coordinate location (P625) == * {{coord|35.000305|135.888094}}Kokugakuin University Shrine database == official website (P856) == * http://www.iwaijinzya.org/ ** Language of work: {{ill|Q5287|ltq=Q5287|qid=Q5287|1=Japanese language}} == image (P18) == * Iwai Shrine.jpg == Kokugakuin University Digital Museum entry ID (P13677) == * 181783 ** P3831: {{ill|Kokugakuin University Shrine database|lt=Kokugakuin University Shrine database|qid=Q135159299|1=Kokugakuin University Shrine database}} == country (P17) == * {{ill|Q17|ltq=Q17|qid=Q17|1=Japan}} == Corporate Number (Japan) (P3225) == * 3160005001025 == street address (P6375) == * {'text': '滋賀県大津市西の庄15-16', 'language': 'ja'} [[JA:石坐神社]] {{wikidata link|Q85882705}} == Japanese content == {{Shinto shrine |Name = Iwai Shrine |Image = [[File:Iwai Shrine.jpg|270px]]
Torii |Location = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōtsu City|Q202907|qid=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}} Nishi-no-shō 15-16 |Coordinates = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |Enshrined deity = Ōmi Iwai Ōkami |Sacred object = |Shrine rank = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}} (Minor)
Former {{ill|lt=Village shrine|Q134917284|qid=Q134917284|1=Son-sha}} |Established = Unknown |Chief priest = |Main hall = Three-bay {{ill|lt=nagare-zukuri|Q210684|qid=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |Alternative names = |Pilgrimage affiliation = |Annual festival = {{ill|lt=May 3|Q2547|qid=Q2547|1=May 3}} (Gosha Festival) |Rituals = |Map = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Iwai Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=12|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=200}} }} '''Iwai Shrine''' (いわいじんじゃ) is a Shinto shrine located in {{ill|lt=Ōtsu City|Q202907|qid=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|qid=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|qid=Q134917286}}, and its former shrine rank was that of a village shrine. === Enshrined Deities === ;Comprehensive divine name * Ōmi Iwai Ōkami ;East Hall * Hachidai Ryūō Shrine ** Toyotama-hiko-no-kami, sea deity (maternal grandfather of Emperor Jinmu) ** {{ill|lt=Hikoimasu no Ō|Q11487910|qid=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}} (third son of Emperor Kaika) * Shōrei Tennō Shrine ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}} (Amenome Oshi Hirakiwake no Sumeramikoto) ** {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōbun|Q357242|qid=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo|1=Emperor Kōbun}} (Emperor Kōbun) ** Iga no Yakatoko-hime (birth mother of Prince Ōtomo) ;West Hall * Tenjō Shōrai Shrine ** Heavenly and earthly deities * Ōmi Ryūō Shrine ** Ōmi Ryūō deity * Fukutoku Shrine ** The Seven Lucky Gods of Iwai * Ujigami Shrine ** Satabiko no Kami ;Inner Shrine * Goryōden Shrine ** Iwai Ōkami ** Hachidai Ryūō deity === History === The date of the shrine’s founding is unknown. According to shrine tradition, Haruta no Muraji, the first provincial governor (kuni no miyatsuko) of the Ōmi Province government established at Seta, buried his ancestor from four generations earlier, {{ill|lt=Hikoimasu no Ō|Q11487910|qid=Q11487910|1=Hikoimasu no Ōkimi}}, at {{ill|lt=Chausuyama Kofun (Ōtsu City)|Q27926911|qid=Q27926911|lt=Mount Chausu|1=Chausuyama Kofun (Ōtsu)}}, and enshrined the mountain behind it, Mount Goryōden, as a {{ill|lt=shintai mountain|Q1595289|qid=Q1595289|1=Sacred mountains}}. This is said to be the origin of the shrine.{{Cite web|Japanese|url=http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=67|title=Shrine Introduction|accessdate=2019-07-21|publisher=Shiga Prefecture Association of Shinto Shrines}} After the {{ill|lt=Jinshin War|Q2378252|qid=Q2378252|1=Jinshin War}}, the shrine outwardly enshrined the {{ill|lt=Hachidai Ryūō|Q11390736|qid=Q11390736|1=Eight Great Dragon Kings}}, but secretly venerated {{ill|lt=Emperor Tenji|Q314802|qid=Q314802|1=Emperor Tenji}}, {{ill|lt=Emperor Kōbun|Q357242|qid=Q357242|lt=Prince Ōtomo|1=Emperor Kōbun}}, and {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Iga no Uneme Yakako|1=UNKNOWN}}.{{Sfn|“Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names” Editorial Committee|1979|p=765}} === Cultural Properties === ;Important Cultural Properties * Wooden seated statue of Amenome Oshi Hirakiwake no Mikoto – Heian period * Wooden seated statue of Iga no Uneme Yakako-hime – Heian period * Wooden seated statue of Emperor Kōbun – Heian period * Wooden seated statue of Hikoimasu no Ō – Kamakura period ;Shiga Prefecture Designated Cultural Properties * Main hall of Iwai Shrine – Built in {{ill|lt=Bunei|Q1010103|qid=Q1010103|1=Bun'ei}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1266|Q5517|qid=Q5517|1=1266}}), as confirmed by a copy of a ridge tag made in {{ill|lt=Genroku|Q1325001|qid=Q1325001|1=Genroku}} 3 ({{ill|lt=1692|Q7685|qid=Q7685|1=1692}}).{{sfn|Ōtsu City Museum of History|2004|p=26}} It is a three-bay nagare-zukuri structure with {{ill|lt=hiwadabuki|Q90373477|qid=Q90373477|1=cypress bark roof}} (cypress bark roofing).{{sfn|Ōtsu City Museum of History|2004|p=26}} === Access === * 5-minute walk from “{{ill|lt=Nishiki Station|Q7040593|qid=Q7040593|lt=Nishiki|1=Nishiki Station}}” on the Keihan Ishiyama–Sakamoto Line * 9-minute walk from “{{ill|lt=Zeze Station|Q1042692|qid=Q1042692|lt=Zeze|1=Zeze Station}}” on the Tōkaidō Main Line (JR Biwako Line) * 3-minute walk from “Nishi-no-shō (in front of Ōtsu Prince Hotel)” bus stop on Keihan Bus or Ōmi Railway (bus) === Notes === {{reflist}} === References === * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names, Vol. 25: Shiga Prefecture|date=1979-05-04|year=1979|publisher=Kadokawa Shoten|ref=harv|author=“Kadokawa Dictionary of Japanese Place Names” Editorial Committee}} * {{Cite book|Japanese|title=Ōtsu: History and Culture — Rediscovering Local History!|date=2004-10-01|year=2004|publisher=Ōtsu City|author=Ōtsu City Museum of History|ref=harv}} === External Links === * [http://www.iwaijinzya.org/ Iwai Shrine] – Official website * [https://www.biwako-visitors.jp/spot/detail/129 Iwai Shrine | Shiga Tourism Information] {{shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Iwai Shrine (Q85882705)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iwai Shrine (Q85882705)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Iwai Shrine (Q85882705)]][[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:大津市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Shiga Prefecture designated tangible cultural property]]
